title: Imaging Mass Cytometry Data Acquisition
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"a2g3l","text":"This SOP describes the image acquisition with an Imaging Mass Cytometer (IMC or","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"4q70d","text":"Hyperion).","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"a2qgb","text":"Prior to this protocol the IMC needs to be fully tuned to specifications (see","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"2kbdl","text":"“HuBMAP_Protocol_4_IMC_Tuning.pdf”). Additionally, every batch of samples stained","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"7c3dt","text":"with the same antibody-mix requires the acquisition of a compensation slide for spill over","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"4to6c","text":"correction (see “HuBMAP_Protocol_3_Compensation_Slide_Preparation.pdf” \u0026","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"s7jm","text":"“HuBMAP_Protocol_6_IMC_Compensation_Slide_Acquisition.pdf”).","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
guidelines: {"blocks":[{"key":"91bjp","text":"IMC: Imaging Mass Cytometer or Hyperion (Commercial name by","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"3c9mp","text":"manufacturer).","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"bmhdp","text":"FFPE: Formalin-fixed, paraffin-embedded.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"425a7","text":"Slide: Refers to a microscopy glass slide containing one or multiple","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"e2ggu","text":"Samples.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"2kkup","text":"Sample: Refers to either a clinical FFPE tissue section from a patient or a","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"cvbjs","text":"cell pellet cytoblock FFPE control sample on a glass slide.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"c7792","text":"Unstained sample: Refers to a sample prior to antibody staining.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"1ls2c","text":"Stained sample: Refers to a sample after antibody staining.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"b5fau","text":"ROI: Region of interest","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
materials_text: {"blocks":[{"key":"9UY3W","text":"MATERIALS","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":null,"entityRanges":null,"data":null},{"key":"gCMMx","text":" ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":null,"entityRanges":[{"key":0,"offset":0,"length":1}],"data":null},{"key":"wEl2N","text":" ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":null,"entityRanges":[{"key":1,"offset":0,"length":1}],"data":null},{"key":"RBT4S","text":" ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":null,"entityRanges":[{"key":2,"offset":0,"length":1}],"data":null}],"entityMap":{"0":{"type":"reagents","mutability":"","data":{"id":415572,"guid":"42560708341F11EE9D4302C0B41BC903","name":"IMC (software 7.0 and higher)","mol_weight":0,"sku":"","url":"","cas_number":"","rrid":"","vendor":{"id":291,"name":"Fluidigm","link":"https://www.fluidigm.com/","is_requested":false,"image":{"source":"/img/vendors/placeholder.png","placeholder":"/img/vendors/placeholder.png"}},"is_public":true,"can_edit":false}},"1":{"type":"reagents","mutability":"","data":{"id":415574,"guid":"425607AB341F11EE9D4302C0B41BC903","name":"Flat-bed scanner with custom sample holder","mol_weight":0,"sku":"","url":"","cas_number":"","rrid":"","vendor":{"id":1,"name":"Contributed by users","link":"https://www.protocols.io","is_requested":false,"image":{"source":"/img/vendors/1.png","placeholder":"/img/vendors/1.png"}},"is_public":true,"can_edit":false}},"2":{"type":"reagents","mutability":"","data":{"id":415575,"guid":"425607FD341F11EE9D4302C0B41BC903","name":"Slide containing stained samples","mol_weight":0,"sku":"","url":"","cas_number":"","rrid":"","vendor":{"id":1,"name":"Contributed by users","link":"https://www.protocols.io","is_requested":false,"image":{"source":"/img/vendors/1.png","placeholder":"/img/vendors/1.png"}},"is_public":true,"can_edit":false}}}}
steps: 1. Attach the flat-bed scanner to a computer.

1. Use the flat-bed scanner with the custom sample holder and load up to 12 slides to be acquired. 2. Scan the slides to .jpg at a resolution of 600 dpi and save the image. The low resolution overview scanning of each slide can be performed on any computer.

1. Insert slide into the IMC. 2. Under “Data acquisition” create a new Empty12AF file. 3. Make sure that the autofocus pins in the software are not located above the hydrophobic marker pen. This may lead to the creation of a false focal plane image of the slide and result in decreased ablation efficiency. 4. Save the mcd file in the data drive (“E”) as a new folder. 5. Create panorama images of the tissue samples. Note: Panoramas should not be larger than 10’000 x 10’000 pixels. This requires a running, fully tuned IMC.

1. Locate the areas of interest identified in IHC staining in the panorama. 2. Set up an ROI for the region(s) of interest with a defined area(s) (e.g. 1000 x 1000 pixels). 3. In AirLab download the relevant panel (in options select “for CyTOF 2”). Save it to the folder where the mcd file is located. 4. Select all ROIs and under “Template” import the panel downloaded from AirLab.Scroll to the bottom of the list and select the imported panel. In the panel selection pane, set the ablation energy to 2.5 dB and the Ablation Frequency to 400 Hz. 5. Double check that the right template was selected and that Ir191 and Ir193 are included. 6. Hit select template. 7. Set the DV Opt parameter to 4 h. 8. Check the “Generate Text File” box. 9. Hit start. (1 mm2 at 400 Hz should roughly take 1h). Once the panel has been downloaded from AirLab and added to the first acquisition it is available in the IMC software. It will then not be necessary to repeat this step afterwards and it will also not be necessary to save the panel.conf to every single folder that is created for each slideID.
creator: Marda Jorgensen
